metadata.title: DOCSAM: Unified Document Image Segmentation via Query Decomposition and Heterogeneous Mixed Learning
metadata.description: DocSAM is a transformer-based unified framework for diverse document image segmentation tasks, including layout analysis, text detection, and table structure recognition, trained on heterogeneous datasets to enhance generalization and efficiency.

sections.title: Introduction and Overview
sections.content: Xiao-Hui Li, Fei Yin, Cheng-Lin Liu — MAIS, Institute of Automation of Chinese Academy of Sciences; School of Artificial Intelligence, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences. DocSAM unifies various document image segmentation tasks into a single model using semantic and instance query decomposition, enabling joint training across heterogeneous datasets.

sections.title: Model Architecture
sections.content: DocSAM consists of four main modules: Vision Backbone (Swin-Transformer), Deformable Encoder, Sentence-BERT for semantic query embedding, and Hybrid Query Decoder (HQD). The HQD enables interaction between semantic and instance queries through multi-head attention and cross-attention with multi-scale image features, producing masks, class predictions, and bounding boxes.

sections.title: Datasets and Evaluation
sections.content: DocSAM is evaluated on 48 heterogeneous datasets spanning Document Layout Analysis, Ancient/Handwritten Document Segmentation, Table Structure Recognition, and Scene Text Detection. Key metrics include mAP, mAP_b, mAF for instance segmentation and mIoU for semantic segmentation. Ablation studies and fine-tuning results demonstrate its strong generalization and transfer learning capabilities.

sections.title: Training and Ablation Studies
sections.content: DocSAM employs heterogeneous mixed learning, curriculum learning, and instance query selection to improve convergence and efficiency. Ablation studies confirm the importance of query interaction, masked attention, and model capacity. SimMIM pretraining unexpectedly degraded performance, while freezing Sentence-BERT had minimal impact.

sections.title: Qualitative Results and Limitations
sections.content: DocSAM shows strong qualitative performance across modern/historical layout analysis, table structure recognition, and scene text detection. However, it struggles with logical layout analysis requiring multimodal cues and complex scene texts (curved, blurred, occluded). Failure cases include over-segmentation and imprecise bounding boxes in dense regions.
